To deploy **AEWorks Manager** successfully on Vercel and ensure it functions perfectly with its Google Cloud integration, follow these specific steps:

### 1. Preparation & Push to Git
Vercel works best with a Git-based workflow.
1.  **Initialize Git**: If you haven't already, run `git init` in your project folder.
2.  **Commit Code**: Run `git add .` and `git commit -m "Initial commit for Vercel"`.
3.  **Push to GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket**: Create a new repository and push your code there.

### 2. Vercel Deployment
1.  **Import Project**: Go to [Vercel](https://vercel.com), click **"Add New"** > **"Project"**, and import your repository.
2.  **Configure Framework**: Vercel should auto-detect **Vite**. If not, select it manually.
3.  **Build Settings**:
    *   **Build Command**: `npm run build`
    *   **Output Directory**: `dist`
4.  **Environment Variables**: If you eventually integrate the Gemini API or other private services, add `API_KEY` here. For now, the app uses a default Google Client ID, but you can add it as an env var if you wish to override it.
5.  **Deploy**: Click **"Deploy"**.

### 3. CRITICAL: Update Google Cloud Console
Because this app uses Google Drive Sync, you must authorize your new Vercel URL:
1.  Go to the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/).
2.  Navigate to **APIs & Services > Credentials**.
3.  Edit your **OAuth 2.0 Client ID**.
4.  Under **Authorized JavaScript origins**, add your Vercel production URL (e.g., `https://aeworks-manager.vercel.app`).
5.  **Save changes** (Note: It may take 5–10 minutes for Google to apply these changes).

---

### Post-Deployment Testing Checklist

To ensure "smooth" operation, test the following in order:

#### A. Routing & Persistence Test
*   **Refresh Check**: Navigate to a module (like Payroll) and refresh the browser. The `vercel.json` we added ensures you don't get a 404 error.
*   **LocalStorage Check**: Create a dummy project, close the tab, and reopen it. The project should still be there (stored in `localStorage`).

#### B. Google Drive Sync Test (The most important)
1.  **Login**: Click "Cloud Bridge" on the login screen.
2.  **Authorize**: Click "Authorize Drive Bridge". If you get an "Origin Mismatch" error, verify your Vercel URL is exactly matched in the GCP Console (including `https://` and no trailing slash).
3.  **Sync**: Ensure the "Master Vault Bonded" notification appears. This confirms the Vercel app can communicate with Google's APIs.

#### C. Mobile Responsiveness
*   Open the Vercel URL on a smartphone.
*   Test the **Status Bar** and **Toolbar** horizontal scrolling.
*   Ensure the **Login Modal** doesn't cut off on smaller screens.

#### D. PDF/Print Test
*   Navigate to **Invoicing**.
*   Click **Export / Print**. 
*   Verify that the "Print-only" styles hide the sidebars and navigation, showing only the professional invoice layout.

### Troubleshooting Vercel Issues
*   **White Screen on Load**: Check the browser console (F12). If you see `process is not defined`, ensure `vite.config.ts` has the `define: { 'process.env': process.env }` block.
*   **Build Failures**: Ensure you didn't include a `src/` prefix in your `index.html` script imports; Vercel expects the root structure provided.

### Steps to set Environment Variables in Vercel:

1.  Open your project in the **Vercel Dashboard**.
2.  Go to **Settings** > **Environment Variables**.
3.  Add the following:
    *   **`VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`**: Paste your Google OAuth Client ID here.
    *   **`API_KEY`**: Paste your Gemini/Google AI API Key here (to be ready for AI features).
4.  **Re-deploy** your project for these changes to take effect.

By setting these, your app will automatically use the production Client ID when running on Vercel, but will fall back to the development ID when you run it locally!

The main constraint I'm addressing is providing clear, actionable steps, as requested. I've also checked existing UI elements, and I'll include the guide.


**Defining User Testing Procedure**

I'm now fully immersed in developing a detailed, user-friendly testing guide for the Google Drive synchronization feature. My primary aim is to translate technical steps into natural language, empowering users to independently verify the app's functionality. I've also identified a key UX enhancement: updating the Toolbar to provide clearer feedback on the "Last Sync" time, enhancing the user experience during testing. I'm structuring the guide into distinct phases, mirroring the sync process for clarity and conciseness.

To test the synchronization capability of AEWorks Manager, you are essentially testing the **"Cloud Bridge"** which connects your local browser storage to a master JSON vault on your Google Drive.

Follow these exact steps to verify that data is moving correctly between your local machine and the cloud.

### Phase 1: Google Cloud Setup (The "Phone Line")
Before testing, you must ensure the "line" is open between Vercel and Google.
1.  **Project Choice**: Go to the [GCP Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/).
2.  **Enable API**: Search for **"Google Drive API"** and click **Enable**.
3.  **Test User**: Go to **APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen**. Under **"Test users"**, add your personal Gmail address. *The sync will fail if your email isn't on this list while the app is in "Testing" mode.*
4.  **Client ID**: Go to **Credentials > Create Credentials > OAuth client ID**.
    *   **Type**: Web Application.
    *   **Authorized JavaScript Origins**: Add `http://localhost:3000` (for local tests) and your Vercel URL (e.g., `https://aeworks-manager.vercel.app`).
5.  **Copy Client ID**: You'll need this string for the next phase.

### Phase 2: Linking the Bridge
1.  **Open the App**: Launch your deployed Vercel URL.
2.  **Logout**: If you are logged in, log out to see the Login screen.
3.  **Cloud Bridge Tab**: Click the **"Cloud Bridge"** tab at the top of the login modal.
4.  **Configuration**: 
    *   Paste your **Google Client ID** into the text field.
    *   Click **"Authorize Drive Bridge"**.
5.  **Consent**: A Google popup will appear. Select your account and click **"Allow"** (you may have to click "Advanced > Go to AEWorks" if prompted about an unverified app).
6.  **Success Check**: You should see a notification: **"Master Vault Bonded"**.

### Phase 3: The "First Push" Test
1.  **Login**: Use the default credentials (ID: `SuperAdmin`, Token: `masterPassword123`).
2.  **Create Data**: Go to the **Project Board** and create a new project called `"Sync Test Alpha"`. Add one framing item.
3.  **Commit**: Click the **"Commit"** button in the Toolbar. This triggers a background push to Drive.
4.  **Verify on Drive**: Open your [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/) in a new tab. Look for a file named `AEWORKS_MASTER_VAULT.json`. If it's there, the push succeeded.

### Phase 4: The "Cross-Device" Test (The True Test)
1.  **Simulate New Device**: Open the app in an **Incognito Window** or a different browser (like Edge or Firefox).
2.  **Repeat Link**: Go to **Cloud Bridge**, paste the same **Client ID**, and **Authorize**.
3.  **The Pull**: Instead of just bonding, the system will detect the existing `AEWORKS_MASTER_VAULT.json` on your Drive.
4.  **Verify**: Log in. If the project `"Sync Test Alpha"` is already in your project list, **the synchronization is working perfectly.**

### Phase 5: Conflict Resolution (Timestamp Test)
1.  **Machine A**: Change the project name to `"Sync Test Alpha - Updated"`. Click **Commit**.
2.  **Machine B**: Refresh the page or click the **"Drive Connected"** button in the toolbar to trigger a manual pull.
3.  **Result**: Machine B should now show the updated name.

---

I've updated the `Toolbar` and `db.ts` slightly to provide better visual feedback during this testing process, specifically adding a "Last Sync" timestamp so you can see exactly when the data last moved.
